name: Viktor Orbán
role: Prime Minister of Hungary 2010–2026, Fidesz party founder and chairman
quote: We are building an illiberal state, a non-liberal state. I don't think that our European Union membership precludes us from building an illiberal new state based on national foundations.

name: Péter Magyar
role: Leader, Tisza Party (TISZA — Tisztelet és Szabadság); Prime Minister-elect as of April 2026
quote: Hungary can finally become a normal European country. We will restore the rule of law, join the European Public Prosecutor's Office, and end the era of systemic corruption.

name: Gergely Gulyás
role: Minister heading PM's Office 2018–2026; Fidesz parliamentary group leader (opposition) from May 2026
quote: We accept the result of the election. Fidesz will be a constructive opposition and will return to serve Hungary's national interest.

name: Péter Szijjártó
role: Foreign Minister of Hungary 2014–2026; principal defender of Hungary's Eastern Opening policy
quote: Hungary will not supply weapons to Ukraine. We will not jeopardize the security of Hungarians for a war that is not ours to fight. Diplomacy is the only way out.

name: Katalin Novák
role: President of Hungary 2022–February 2024; resigned over pardoning a convicted child abuse accomplice
quote: I made a mistake when I signed the pardon. I ask for forgiveness for signing a pardon decision that could be misunderstood, and that undermined my ability to fulfill my role.

name: Tamás Sulyok
role: President of Hungary from March 2024; former Constitutional Court President; convened inaugural parliament for May 9
quote: I call all parties elected to the new National Assembly to productive cooperation in Hungary's democratic and European future.

name: László Kövér
role: Speaker of the National Assembly 2010–2026; one of Orbán's closest allies since the Fidesz founding generation
quote: Those who would turn Hungary into a migration corridor serving global capital are enemies of the Hungarian nation, regardless of their title or nationality.

name: Judit Varga
role: Justice Minister 2019–2024; EU Affairs Minister 2018–2019; MEP elected 2024. Ex-wife of Péter Magyar; resigned over pardon scandal
quote: I countersigned the pardon in the performance of my ministerial duties. Given the political circumstances, I step aside from all political positions to protect the integrity of my party.

name: Balázs Orbán
role: Political Director to PM Viktor Orbán 2021–2026 (no family relation); architect of the 'Hungarian model' messaging internationally
quote: Hungarian sovereignty is not negotiable. Budapest will not become a pawn in the great power game of Brussels federalists and Washington neoconservatives.

name: Gergely Karácsony
role: Mayor of Budapest from October 2019; Párbeszéd/Greens politician; defeated incumbent Fidesz-backed Mayor Tarlós
quote: Budapest is European. Budapest is free. This city will remain a home for everyone, regardless of where they come from or who they love.

name: Klára Dobrev
role: MEP (DK — Democratic Coalition); Vice President of the European Parliament; wife of former PM Ferenc Gyurcsány
quote: Hungary's democracy has been systematically dismantled brick by brick over 16 years. Every EU institution that remained silent was complicit in what happened here.

name: Péter Márki-Zay
role: United opposition PM candidate in 2022 election; Mayor of Hódmezővásárhely; conservative who ran against Orbán
quote: Hungary needs real change, not just a change of faces. The whole system built over 12 years must be dismantled and replaced with genuine rule of law.

name: Ursula von der Leyen
role: President of the European Commission 2019–present; led conditionality mechanism and frozen funds negotiations with both Orbán and Magyar
quote: The rule-of-law conditionality mechanism protects every EU taxpayer. European values are not a menu from which governments can pick and choose.

name: Věra Jourová
role: Vice President of the European Commission for Values and Transparency 2019–present; led rule-of-law monitoring of Hungary
quote: What happened in Hungary is a democracy that fell ill. Institutions were hollowed out from the inside. The EU was too slow to respond, and we must learn from that failure.

name: Manfred Weber
role: Chairman of the European People's Party (EPP); German MEP (CSU); expelled Fidesz from EPP in 2021
quote: Fidesz crossed every red line — attacking universities, the free press, the independence of courts. The EPP cannot remain the political home of a party that rejects European values.

name: George Soros
role: Hungarian-American philanthropist; Open Society Foundations founder; principal target of Orbán's foreign agent campaign
quote: The repressive measures Orbán has taken are more comprehensive and have been deployed more successfully than in any other country. He has turned Hungary into a kleptocracy.

name: Zoltán Kovács
role: Secretary of State for International Communication and Relations under Orbán; chief English-language spokesperson for the government
quote: Brussels elites are trying to force a failed multicultural experiment on Hungary. We stand for the right of Hungarians to decide who comes into their country — not George Soros, not the UN.

name: Didier Reynders
role: European Commissioner for Justice 2019–2024 (Belgium); led Article 7 hearings and rule-of-law monitoring of Hungary
quote: Hungary's rule-of-law situation requires structural and systemic remedies, not cosmetic changes. The milestones we have set are the minimum required by the Treaties.

name: Tibor Navracsics
role: Minister without Portfolio for Territorial Development and EU Funds 2022–2024; former EU Commissioner; key figure in frozen funds negotiations
quote: Hungary has fulfilled every specific milestone set by the Commission. We expect that political considerations will not prevent the disbursement of funds Hungarian citizens are entitled to.

name: Anna Donáth
role: MEP (Momentum/Renew); Hungarian liberal opposition figure; consistent voice in the European Parliament on Hungary's democratic backsliding
quote: I have been shouting for six years in the European Parliament that Hungary is not a democracy. I was called hysterical, exaggerating. Today the Hungarian people vindicated us all.
